root_text.sn51.1:1.1: “Cattārome, bhikkhave, iddhipādā bhāvitā bahulīkatā apārā pāraṁ gamanāya saṁvattanti.
root_text.sn51.1:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu chandasamādhippadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti,
root_text.sn51.1:1.7: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro iddhipādā bhāvitā bahulīkatā apārā pāraṁ gamanāya saṁvattantī”ti.

translation_text.sn51.1:1.1: “Mendicants, when these four bases of psychic power are developed and cultivated they lead to going from the near shore to the far shore.
translation_text.sn51.1:1.3: It’s when a mendicant develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to enthusiasm, and active effort.
translation_text.sn51.1:1.4: They develop the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to energy, and active effort.
translation_text.sn51.1:1.5: They develop the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to mental development, and active effort.
translation_text.sn51.1:1.6: They develop the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to inquiry, and active effort.
translation_text.sn51.1:1.7: When these four bases of psychic power are developed and cultivated they lead to going from the near shore to the far shore.”
